info.title: Zadarma API
info.description:
Zadarma API.

**Base URL:** `https://api.zadarma.com`

**Authorization:** Each request must include an `Authorization` header in the format `userKey:signature`. The signature is computed as:
1. Sort request params alphabetically by key
2. Build query string (RFC 1738)
3. Concatenate: `method_path + query_string + md5(query_string)`
4. `signature = base64(HMAC-SHA1(concatenated_string, secret_key))`

**Response format:** JSON by default; add `format=xml` to get XML.

**Rate limits:** 100 requests/min general; 3 requests/min for statistics methods. HTTP 429 is returned when the limit is exceeded.

tags.name: Info
tags.description: Account information: balance, call rates, timezone, price plan details, HLR lookup, and reference lists.

tags.name: SIP
tags.description: SIP number management: listing, status, CallerID, call forwarding, creation and password management.

tags.name: Statistics
tags.description: Call statistics: overall, PBX, callback widget, and incoming calls.

tags.name: PBX
tags.description: PBX configuration: call forwarding, recordings, hold music, call info webhooks, and PBX creation.

tags.name: PBX Extensions
tags.description: PBX extension management: listing, status, info, recording settings, creation and passwords.

tags.name: PBX IVR
tags.description: IVR sound files and scenario management.

tags.name: Speech Recognition
tags.description: Obtaining and initiating speech-to-text recognition for PBX call recordings.

tags.name: Virtual Numbers
tags.description: Management of virtual (direct) phone numbers: info, ordering, prolongation, forwarding, SMS settings and channel configuration.

tags.name: Documents
tags.description: Management of document groups required for virtual number ordering and verification.

tags.name: Reseller
tags.description: Reseller account management: sub-user registration, balance transfers, phone number management, and API key control.

tags.name: SMS
tags.description: Sending SMS messages and managing templates and sender IDs.

tags.name: WebRTC
tags.description: WebRTC widget management: key generation, widget creation, domain allowlisting, and deletion.

tags.name: eSIM
tags.description: eSIM device catalogue, data package listing, and order management.

tags.name: Verify
tags.description: Phone and email verification via four channels: sms, call_code, email, call_button.

ApiKeyAuth.description:
Authorization header format: `userKey:signature`.
Keys are generated in your personal account at https://my.zadarma.com/api/.
Signature algorithm: `base64(HMAC-SHA1(method + queryString + md5(queryString), secretKey))`
where params are sorted alphabetically and encoded with http_build_query (RFC 1738).

get.description: Returns the current balance and currency of the authenticated user's account.

get.summary: Call rate for a phone number
get.description: Returns the call rate for a given phone number under the user's current price plan. Optionally accepts a CallerID to compute the rate as if calling from that number.

number.description: Destination phone number in international format.

caller_id.description: CallerID used to make the call (optional). International format.

prefix.description: Matched billing prefix for the number.

description.description: Human-readable description of the call destination.

get.description: Returns the timezone configured for the authenticated user's account, along with the current Unix timestamp and formatted datetime in that timezone.

datetime.description: Current date and time in the user's timezone, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

get.description: Returns detailed information about the user's currently active price plan, including usage statistics and the plan scheduled for the next billing period.

tariff_name.description: Name of the user's current price plan.

is_active.description: Whether the current price plan is active.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the price plan.

used_seconds_mobile.description: Price plan seconds used on calls to mobile numbers.

used_seconds_fix.description: Price plan seconds used on calls to landline numbers.

used_seconds_speech_recognition.description: Price plan seconds used for speech recognition.

tariff_id_for_next_period.description: Price plan ID scheduled for the next billing period.

tariff_for_next_period.description: Name of the price plan for the next billing period.

get.description: Initiates a callback: the system first calls the `from` number, then connects it to the `to` number. If `predicted` flag is set, the system calls `to` first and only bridges the call if `to` answers.

from.description: Your phone number, SIP number, PBX extension, or PBX scenario to which the callback is made.

to.description: Phone number or SIP number that will be called. International format.

sip.description: SIP user number or PBX extension (e.g. 100) used to make the call (optional). Its CallerID will be used; shown in statistics; recording and prefix dialling apply if enabled.

predicted.description: Predicted callback flag (optional). If specified, the system calls `to` first and only connects to your SIP or phone if the call is answered.

get.description: Initiates a verification call to the specified number. The system calls `to` from `caller_id` and plays the given `code` as digits.

caller_id.description: Number displayed during the call (CallerID). Only numbers connected to Zadarma are accepted.

to.description: Phone number or SIP that will be called for verification. International format.

code.description: Verification code that will be played during the call. Digits only, maximum 20 characters.

lang.description: Language for voicing the code (optional). If empty, the client account language is used if available in system languages.

from.description: CallerID number used for the verification call.

to.description: Phone number or SIP that was called.

lang.description: Language used to voice the code.

time.description: Unix timestamp of when the verification call was initiated.

post.description:
Submits one or more phone numbers for HLR lookup to verify if they are active, along with their network, porting and roaming status.

- **Single number:** result returned immediately (Response 1).
- **Multiple numbers:** only `{"status":"success"}` is returned (Response 2); full results are sent asynchronously to the configured webhook URL or account email.

**Attention:** configure the HLR page in your personal account before using this method.

numbers.description: One or more phone numbers in international format

200.description:
Response 1 (single number): full HLR result returned immediately.
Response 2 (multiple numbers): acknowledgement only — result will be sent to webhook/email.

ported.description: Whether the number has been ported to another operator.

roaming.description: Whether the subscriber is currently roaming.

errorDescription.description: HLR lookup error description; 'No Error' if successful.

ported.description: Whether the number has been ported to another operator.

roaming.description: Whether the subscriber is currently roaming.

errorDescription.description: HLR lookup error description; 'No Error' if successful.

get.description: Returns the list of currencies supported by Zadarma for pricing and billing.

currencies.description: List of supported ISO 4217 currency codes.

get.description: Returns the list of language codes available in the Zadarma personal account interface.

languages.description: List of ISO 639-1 language codes available in the personal account.

get.description: Returns all available Zadarma price plans in the specified currency, including standard tariffs and package (bundled minutes) tariffs.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code in which plan costs should be returned.
Currency in which plan costs are expressed.

name.description: Display name of the price plan.

cost.description: Monthly cost in the requested currency.

tariffs.description: Individual plans within this package group.

name.description: Display name of the package plan.

cost.description: Monthly cost in the requested currency.

cost_annual.description: Annual cost in the requested currency (when paid yearly).

get.description: Returns the list of all SIP numbers registered to the authenticated user, along with the number of remaining SIPs that can still be created.

display_name.description: Display name of the SIP number.

lines.description: Number of simultaneous lines available for this SIP.

left.description: Number of remaining SIPs that can be created, based on the user's balance and existing SIP count.

get.description: Returns the current online (registration) status of the specified user's SIP number.

is_online.description: Online status of the SIP number: 'true' if registered, 'false' if offline.

put.summary: Change CallerID for a SIP number
put.description: Changes the CallerID phone number displayed when making outgoing calls from the specified SIP number. The new number must be from the list of confirmed or purchased phone numbers.

id.description: The SIP ID for which the CallerID needs to be changed.

number.description: New CallerID phone number in international format. Must be from the list of confirmed or purchased numbers.

sip.description: SIP number ID for which the CallerID was changed.

new_caller_id.description: The newly set CallerID phone number.

get.description: Returns the current call forwarding configuration for all user's SIP numbers, or for a specific SIP if the optional `id` parameter is provided.

id.description: Filter by a specific SIP ID (optional). If omitted, all SIP numbers are returned.

info.description: List of call forwarding configurations per SIP number.

condition.description: Call forwarding condition: 'always' — forward all calls; 'unavailable' — forward only when unanswered or SIP is offline.

destination.description: Forwarding destination type: 'phone' — to a phone number; 'pbx' — to a PBX.

destination_value.description: The forwarding target: a phone number or PBX ID, depending on the destination type.

put.summary: Change call forwarding on SIP number
put.description:
Updates call forwarding settings for the specified SIP number.

Two mutually exclusive use cases, distinguished by which parameters are provided:

**Case 1 — Switch forwarding on/off** (provide `id` + `status`):
Enables or disables call forwarding without changing its destination. Returns the resulting `current_status`.

**Case 2 — Change forwarding destination** (provide `id` + `type` + `number`):
Sets a new forwarding target (phone number). Optionally pass `condition` to control when forwarding is triggered. Returns the new `destination`.

id.description: SIP ID whose forwarding status is being changed.

status.description: New forwarding status: 'on' — enable forwarding; 'off' — disable forwarding.

id.description: SIP ID whose forwarding destination is being changed.

type.description: Forwarding type. Currently only 'phone' (forward to a phone number) is supported.

number.description: Phone number to forward calls to, in international format.

condition.description: Forwarding condition (optional, defaults to 'always'): 'always' — forward all calls; 'unavailable' — forward only when the SIP is offline or unanswered.

200.description: Forwarding updated. Case 1 returns `current_status`; Case 2 returns `destination`.

sip.description: SIP ID whose forwarding was updated.

current_status.description: Resulting forwarding status after the operation.

sip.description: SIP ID whose forwarding destination was updated.

400.description: Bad request — missing or invalid parameters.

post.description: Creates a new SIP number for the authenticated user. Optionally, the number can be immediately configured with a phone forwarding destination.

name.description: Display name for the new SIP number.

callerid.description: Phone number to use as CallerID for outgoing calls.

redirect_to_phone.description: Phone number to forward incoming SIP calls to (optional).

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to create a SIP on behalf of a sub-user.

sip.description: The ID of the newly created SIP number.

put.description: Changes the registration password for the specified SIP number. Resellers may use the optional `user_id` parameter to change the password for a SIP belonging to one of their sub-users.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to change a password for a SIP belonging to one of their sub-users.

sip.description: SIP number ID for which the password was changed.

get.description:
Returns call statistics for the authenticated user's SIP numbers.

Maximum period: 1 month. Maximum lines per request: 1000. Use `skip` and `limit` for pagination.

When `cost_only` is specified, returns only the total cost and duration for the period instead of individual call records.

start.description: Start date of the statistics period, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. If omitted, defaults to the start of the current month.

end.description: End date of the statistics period, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. If omitted, defaults to the current date and time.

sip.description: Filter results by a specific SIP number (optional).

cost_only.description: If true, returns only the total cost and seconds for the period instead of individual call records (optional).

type.description: Request type (optional): 'overall' (default) — all calls; 'toll-free' — 800 toll-free numbers only.

skip.description: Number of lines to skip (for pagination with `limit`). Default: 0.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return (for pagination with `skip`). Maximum and default value: 1000.

200.description: Response 1: individual call records. Response 2: cost summary when `cost_only=true`.

start.description: Start date of the returned statistics period.

end.description: End date of the returned statistics period.

sip.description: SIP number used for the call.

from.description: Phone number from which the call was made.

disposition.description: Call status: 'answered' — conversation; 'busy' — busy; 'cancel' — cancelled; 'no answer' — no answer; 'call failed' — failed; 'no money' — no funds/limit exceeded; 'unallocated number' — number does not exist; 'no limit' — limit exceeded; 'no day limit' — day limit exceeded; 'line limit' — line limit exceeded; 'no money, no limit' — limit exceeded.

cost.description: Per-minute call rate to this destination.

billcost.description: Total cost of the billed minutes.

cost.description: Total call cost for the period.

seconds.description: Total call duration in seconds for the period.

get.description:
Returns call statistics for the user's PBX.

**Attention:** Do not poll this method every minute for real-time data — use webhooks to receive notifications at call start and end instead.

Maximum period: 1 month. Maximum lines per request: 1000. Use `skip` and `limit` for pagination.

start.description: Start date of the statistics period, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. If omitted, defaults to the start of the current month.

end.description: End date of the statistics period, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. If omitted, defaults to the current date and time.

version.description: Statistics format version: 2 — new format (recommended); 1 — legacy format.

call_type.description: Filter by call direction (optional): 'in' — incoming; 'out' — outgoing. If omitted, all calls are returned.

skip.description: Number of lines to skip (for pagination with `limit`). Default: 0.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return (for pagination with `skip`). Maximum and default value: 1000.

start.description: Start date of the returned statistics period.

end.description: End date of the returned statistics period.

version.description: Statistics format version used in the response.

call_id.description: Unique call ID. Specified in the call recording filename (unique per recording).

sip.description: SIP number used for the call.

disposition.description: Call status: 'answered' — conversation; 'busy' — busy; 'cancel' — cancelled; 'no answer' — no answer; 'call failed' — failed; 'no money' — no funds/limit exceeded; 'unallocated number' — number does not exist; 'no limit' — limit exceeded; 'no day limit' — day limit exceeded; 'line limit' — line limit exceeded; 'no money, no limit' — limit exceeded.

pbx_call_id.description: Permanent ID of the external call to the PBX. Does not change with scenario/IVR changes.

get.description:
Returns callback widget interaction statistics for the specified period.

Maximum period: 1 month. Maximum lines per request: 1000.

start.description: Start date of the statistics period, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. If omitted, defaults to the start of the current month.

end.description: End date of the statistics period, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. If omitted, defaults to the current date and time.

widget_id.description: Widget ID to filter statistics by (optional). If omitted, statistics from all widgets are returned.

start.description: Start date of the returned statistics period.

end.description: End date of the returned statistics period.

sip.description: SIP number associated with this widget session.

ip.description: IP address of the website visitor.

actions.description: List of events that occurred during the widget session.

kind.description: Event type: 'come' — visitor opened widget page; 'show' — widget form displayed; 'call' — callback request; 'call_request' — delayed callback request; 'rate' — visitor rated the call; 'fail' — visitor reported no callback; 'close' — visitor closed widget form.

date.description: Date and time of the event.

referrer_url.description: URL the visitor came from (only for 'come' event).

url.description: URL of the page with the widget (only for 'come' event).

rate.description: For 'show' — display score; for 'rate' — call rating given by the visitor.

number.description: Phone number entered by the visitor (for 'call', 'call_request', 'rate', 'fail' events).

request_call_date.description: Date and time for the delayed callback requested by the visitor (only for 'call_request' event).

redial.description: Whether a callback was made for the delayed request (only for 'call_request' event).

get.description:
Returns general incoming call statistics for the authenticated user.

Maximum period: 1 month. Maximum lines per request: 1000. Use `skip` and `limit` for pagination.

start.description: Start date of the statistics period, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. If omitted, defaults to the start of the current month.

end.description: End date of the statistics period, format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. If omitted, defaults to the current date and time.

sip.description: Filter by a specific SIP number (optional).

skip.description: Number of lines to skip (for pagination with `limit`). Default: 0.

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return (for pagination with `skip`). Maximum and default value: 1000.

start.description: Start date of the returned statistics period.

end.description: End date of the returned statistics period.

sip.description: SIP number that received the call.

from.description: Phone number from which the call was received.

disposition.description: Call status: 'answered' — conversation; 'busy' — busy; 'cancel' — cancelled; 'no answer' — no answer; 'call failed' — failed; 'no money' — no funds/limit exceeded; 'unallocated number' — number does not exist; 'no limit' — limit exceeded; 'no day limit' — day limit exceeded; 'line limit' — line limit exceeded; 'no money, no limit' — limit exceeded.

get.description: Returns the current call forwarding parameters configured for a PBX extension.

current_status.description: Whether call forwarding is currently enabled.

destination.description: Forwarding destination value: phone number, email, voice menu ID, SIP number, or SIP URI — depending on type.

condition.description: Condition under which forwarding is triggered: always — unconditional; noanswer — when unanswered or no SIP connection.

post.summary: Change call forwarding parameters on PBX extension
post.description:
Sets or changes call forwarding on the specified PBX extension.

Two mutually exclusive use cases, distinguished by `status`:

**Case 1 — Enable forwarding** (`status=on`):** provide `pbx_number`, `status`, `type`, `destination`, `condition`. Optionally `voicemail_greeting` and `greeting_file` when `type=voicemail`.

**Case 2 — Disable forwarding** (`status=off`):** provide only `pbx_number` and `status`.

type.description: Forwarding destination type: 'phone' — to a phone number; 'voicemail' — to voicemail (email); 'menu' — to a voice menu/scenario; 'sip' — to a SIP number or PBX extension; 'sip_uri' — to an external SIP URI server.

destination.description: Forwarding target, depending on `type`: phone number, email address, voice menu/scenario ID, SIP number, PBX extension, or SIP URI address.

condition.description: Forwarding condition: 'always' — forward all calls; 'noanswer' — forward only when the extension is unanswered or offline.

voicemail_greeting.description: Voicemail greeting notification type. Only used when `type=voicemail`: 'no' — no greeting; 'standart' — default greeting; 'own' — custom file.

greeting_file.description: Custom greeting audio file (MP3 or WAV, max 5 MB). Only used when `type=voicemail` and `voicemail_greeting=own`. Note: when uploading a file, use multipart/form-data instead of application/x-www-form-urlencoded.

status.description: Must be 'off' to disable forwarding. No other parameters are needed.

200.description: Forwarding settings applied. When disabling, `type`, `destination` and `condition` are omitted.

current_status.description: Resulting forwarding status after the operation.

400.description: Bad request — missing or invalid parameters.

get.description:
Returns a temporary download link for a PBX call recording file.

Provide either `call_id` OR `pbx_call_id` — at least one is required. When `pbx_call_id` is specified, multiple links may be returned.

call_id.description: Unique call ID from the recording filename. Provide this OR `pbx_call_id`.

pbx_call_id.description: Permanent PBX call ID. Provide this OR `call_id`. When specified, multiple links may be returned.

lifetime.description: Link lifetime in seconds (optional). Minimum: 180, maximum: 5184000, default: 1800.

200.description: Response 1: single link (call_id specified). Response 2: multiple links (pbx_call_id specified).

link.description: Temporary download link for the call recording.

lifetime_till.description: Date and time until which the link remains valid.

links.description: List of temporary download links for call recordings.

lifetime_till.description: Date and time until which the links remain valid.

delete.description:
Deletes the recording file for the specified PBX call.

Provide either `call_id` OR `pbx_call_id` — at least one is required. When `pbx_call_id` is specified, multiple files may be deleted.

call_id.description: Unique call ID from the recording filename. Provide this OR `pbx_call_id`.

pbx_call_id.description: Permanent PBX call ID. Provide this OR `call_id`. When specified, multiple files may be deleted.

200.description: Response 1: single file deleted (call_id). Response 2: multiple files deleted (pbx_call_id).

file_name.description: Name of the deleted recording file.

deleted_files.description: List of successfully deleted recording filenames.

failed_files.description: List of filenames that could not be deleted.

post.description: Uploads a custom hold music audio file to replace the default beeps played when a caller is put on hold.

file.description: Audio file to upload (MP3 or WAV format).

id.description: Unique identifier of the uploaded sound file.

name.description: Display name of the uploaded sound file.

put.summary: Switch hold music on or off
put.description: Enables or disables hold music for the PBX, and selects which melody to use.

state.description: Hold music state: 'on' to enable, 'off' to disable.

melody.description: Melody type: 'none' — default system music; 'mymelody' — previously uploaded custom file.

id.description: Unique identifier of the uploaded sound file.

name.description: Display name of the uploaded sound file.

delete.description: Deletes the previously uploaded custom hold music file.

get.description: Returns the current call info webhook URL and notification settings for the PBX.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

url.description: Current webhook URL for call info notifications. Empty string if not set.

notify_answer.description: Notify when the call is answered.

post.summary: Set PBX call info webhook URL
post.description: Sets the webhook URL that receives PBX call info notifications.

url.description: Webhook URL for PBX call info notifications.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

delete.summary: Delete PBX call info webhook URL
delete.description: Removes the webhook URL for PBX call info notifications.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

url.description: Empty string confirming the URL was deleted.

post.summary: Enable or disable PBX call info webhook notifications
post.description: Configures which NOTIFY_* events are sent to the PBX call info webhook URL.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

notify_answer.description: Notify when the call is answered (optional).

post.description: Creates a new PBX for the authenticated user. The first extension '100' will be created automatically with the provided password.

sip_id.description: SIP number to associate with the PBX. If not set, an available SIP is chosen automatically (optional).

password.description: Password for the first PBX extension '100'.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

stop_datetime.description: Date and time when the PBX subscription expires.

get.description: Returns the current webhook URL and enabled notification types for other PBX webhooks (number lookup, call tracking, SMS, speech recognition).

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

url.description: Current webhook URL. Empty string if not set.

post.description: Sets the webhook URL for other PBX notifications (number lookup, call tracking, SMS, speech recognition).

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

delete.description: Removes the webhook URL for other PBX notifications (number lookup, call tracking, SMS, speech recognition).

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

url.description: Empty string confirming the URL was deleted.

post.summary: Enable or disable PBX webhook notification types
post.description: Enables or disables individual webhook notification types: contact updates (number lookup), call tracking, SMS, and speech analytics.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

get.description: Returns the PBX ID and the list of all extension numbers configured for the authenticated user's PBX.

get.summary: Online status of a PBX extension
get.description: Returns the current online (registration) status of the specified PBX extension.

is_online.description: Online status: 'true' if registered, 'false' if offline.

get.description: Returns detailed configuration information for the specified PBX extension.

caller_id_app_change.description: Whether CallerID can be changed from the app.

caller_id_by_direction.description: Whether CallerID is set per destination direction.

lines.description: Number of simultaneous lines for this extension.

ip_restriction.description: IP access restriction. Returns 'false' if not configured.

record_store.description: Call recording storage mode. Returns 'false' if disabled.

record_email.description: Email address for sending call recordings. Returns 'false' if not set.

put.summary: Configure call recording on a PBX extension
put.description: Enables or disables call recording for the specified PBX extension, and optionally configures email delivery and speech recognition.

status.description: 'on' — enable recording; 'off' — disable; 'on_email' — enable email delivery only; 'off_email' — disable email delivery only; 'on_store' — enable cloud storage; 'off_store' — disable cloud storage.

email.description: Email address(es) to send recordings to (optional). Up to 3 addresses, comma-separated.

speech_recognition.description: Speech recognition setting (optional): 'all' — transcribe all calls; 'optional' — transcribe selected calls in statistics; 'off' — disabled.

internal_number.description: PBX extension number that was configured.

email.description: Email address configured for sending recordings.

post.description: Creates one or more new PBX extensions starting from the specified extension number.

start_number.description: Starting extension number (100–999) or 'any' to auto-assign.

return_password.description: If 'true', include generated passwords in the response (optional).

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

numbers.description: List of newly created PBX extension numbers.

get.description: Returns the current password for the specified PBX extension.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

put.description: Changes the registration password for the specified PBX extension.

value.description: New password for the PBX extension.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

number.description: PBX extension number whose password was updated.

put.description: Enables or disables supervisor mode for the specified PBX extension.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

get.description: Returns the list of all sound files uploaded for use in IVR menus.

sounds.description: List of available IVR sound files.

id.description: Unique identifier of the sound file.

name.description: Display name of the sound file.

post.description: Uploads a new audio file to be used in IVR menus.

name.description: Display name for the uploaded sound file.

file.description: Audio file to upload (MP3 or WAV format).

delete.description: Deletes the specified IVR sound file by its ID.

id.description: ID of the IVR sound file to delete.

get.description: Returns the list of IVR menus configured for the user's PBX.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

ivrs.description: List of IVR menus configured for the PBX.

title.description: Display name of the IVR menu.

status.description: Whether the IVR menu is active.

waitexten.description: Seconds to wait for a digit input before timeout.
Seconds to wait for input in auto-responder mode.

working_days.description: Working hours schedule per day of the week. Present only when auto_responder status is 'on'.

is_active.description: Whether this day is a working day.

begin.description: Working hours start time. Present only when is_active is true.

end.description: Working hours end time. Present only when is_active is true.

dinner_status.description: Whether a lunch break is configured for this menu.

dinner_time.description: Lunch break time range. Present only when dinner_status is 'on'.

post.description: Creates a new IVR menu for the user's PBX.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

buy.description: Set to 'true' for paid menu creation (optional).

menu_id.description: Identifier of the newly created IVR menu.

delete.description: Deletes the specified IVR menu by its ID.

menu_id.description: IVR menu ID to delete. Must be greater than 0.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

get.description: Returns the list of scenarios configured for the specified IVR menu.

menu_id.description: IVR menu ID to list scenarios for.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

push_button.description: DTMF digit that triggers this scenario. -1 means the scenario is triggered by default (no digit pressed).

first_sips.description: First ring group — list of PBX extension numbers called first.

second_sips.description: Second ring group — called if first group does not answer.

second_sips_delay.description: Seconds to wait before calling the second ring group.

third_sips.description: Third ring group — called if second group does not answer.

third_sips_delay.description: Seconds to wait before calling the third ring group.

post.description: Creates a new scenario within the specified IVR menu.

push_button.description: Button press activating this scenario. Use -1 for no press, 0–9 for digit keys, 10 for autoresponder, 11–30 for other options.

menu_id.description: IVR menu ID to create the scenario in.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

trigger_to_did_ext_lines.description: Trigger condition: calls to this DID extension line (optional).

trigger_from_clid_numbers.description: Trigger condition: calls from this CallerID number (optional).

menu_id.description: IVR menu identifier to which the scenario belongs.

scenario_id.description: Unique identifier of the newly created IVR scenario.

put.description: Edits an existing IVR scenario. Uses JSON body (not form-urlencoded) to support nested extension arrays.

queue_announce_position.description: Announce queue position to the caller: 0 — disabled (optional).

numbers.description: List of extensions in the queue (optional).

delay.description: Delay in seconds before ringing this extension.

duration.description: Ring duration in seconds for this extension.

delete.description: Deletes the specified IVR scenario by its ID.

scenario_id.description: ID of the IVR scenario to delete.

user_id.description: User ID (optional). Available for resellers only — to act on behalf of a sub-user.

get.description:
Returns the speech recognition result for the specified call recording.

`recognitionStatus` values: `in progress`, `error`, `recognized`, `ready for recognize`, `not available for recognize`.

Use `return` to select result format: `words` (timestamped word array) or `phrases` (full text phrases, default).
Set `alternatives=1` to receive alternative recognition candidates.

call_id.description: Unique call ID from the recording filename.

return.description: Result format (optional): 'words' — timestamped word array; 'phrases' — full text phrases. Default: 'phrases'.

alternatives.description: Return alternative recognition results (optional): 0 — no, 1 — yes. Default: 0.

recognitionStatus.description: Recognition status: 'in progress' — processing; 'error' — failed; 'recognized' — complete; 'ready for recognize' — recording ready; 'not available for recognize' — recording not eligible.

otherLangs.description: Other language codes for which recognition was also performed.

phrases.description: Phrase-level results by channel (present when `return=phrases`, default).

result.description: Full recognized phrase for this channel.

put.summary: Initiate speech recognition for a call recording
put.description:
Initiates speech-to-text recognition for the specified call recording. Requires 'manual recognition' to be enabled in the PBX extension settings beforehand.

After initiating, poll `GET /v1/speech_recognition/` to retrieve the result.

call_id.description: Unique call ID from the recording filename.

lang.description: Recognition language code, e.g. 'en-EN' (optional). If omitted, the account language is used.

get.description:
Returns information about all virtual phone numbers purchased by the user.

Status values: `on` — active; `parking` — disabled but retained 7 days; `checking` — awaiting activation; `checking_upload` — documents required; `reserved_on/checking/checking_upload` — reserved, awaiting payment.

sip.description: SIP number connected to this virtual number.

sip_name.description: Display name of the connected SIP.

stop_date.description: End date of the current payment period.

minutes.description: Total duration of incoming calls for the current month in seconds (revenue numbers only).

type.description: 'common' — active virtual number; 'order' — ordered but not yet connected.

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific virtual number by its phone number.

receive_sms.description: SMS reception status. Null if not supported.

get.description: Returns the current automatic renewal (autoprolongation) status for the specified virtual number.

put.description: Enables or disables automatic renewal for the specified virtual number.

get.summary: Get document or address verification errors
get.description: Returns details about document or address verification errors for the specified document group.

message.example: The passport has expired. It is considered invalid.

get.summary: List countries available for number ordering
get.description: Returns the list of countries from which virtual phone numbers can be ordered.

language.description: Response language code (optional). If not set, personal account language is used.

get.summary: List destinations in a country available for ordering
get.description: Returns available city/destination options in the specified country, with pricing, restrictions, and SMS support info.

direction_id.description: Filter to a specific destination ID (optional).

restrictions.description: Documents/data required to order this number.

receive_sms.description: Whether this number supports SMS reception.

is_toll.description: Whether this is a toll-free number.

put.summary: Set caller name for a virtual number
put.description: Sets the caller name (CNAM) displayed when calling from the specified number. Use Latin letters and digits only, up to 30 characters. Submit an empty `caller_name` to clear.

caller_name.description: Caller name (Latin letters and digits, max 30 chars). Send empty string to clear.

put.summary: Assign number to SIP or enable test mode
put.description: Assigns a virtual number to a SIP number or external SIP URI server, and optionally enables test mode.

sip_id.description: SIP number ID or external SIP URI address.

get.summary: List numbers available for ordering in a direction
get.description: Returns a list of phone numbers currently available to order for the specified destination/city ID.

mask.description: Optional number pattern for filtering (partial match).

id.description: Unique number ID used when placing an order.

post.description: Orders or connects a virtual phone number. Use `number_id` from `GET /v1/direct_numbers/available/{direction_id}/` if you want a specific number, otherwise omit it.

number_id.description: ID of the specific number to order (optional). From GET /direct_numbers/available/{direction_id}/.

documents_group_id.description: ID of the document group to use for verification (optional if no docs required).

purpose.description: Text description of the purpose of number use (optional, if required).

receive_sms.description: Enable SMS reception: 1 = yes (optional, only if number supports SMS).

autorenew_period.description: Auto-renewal period (optional): 'month' or 'year'. Affects subscription fee.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

is_reserved.description: Whether the number is reserved pending payment.

is_activated.description: Whether the number was immediately activated.

post.summary: Prepay a virtual number for additional months
post.description: Extends the subscription period for the specified virtual number by paying for a given number of months in advance.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

stop_date.description: New subscription end date after prolongation.

get.summary: Get incoming channel info for a virtual number
get.description: Returns information about whether the number of incoming channels can be increased, and the associated costs.

direction_id.description: Direction/city ID (at least one of direction_id or number is required).

number.description: Virtual phone number (at least one of direction_id or number is required).

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

put.summary: Increase number of incoming channels on a virtual number
put.description: Sets the number of simultaneous incoming channels for the specified virtual number.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

put.summary: Enable or disable SMS reception on a virtual number
put.description: Enables or disables SMS reception for a virtual number that supports it.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

get.summary: List files in a document group
get.description: Returns the list of uploaded files/documents in the specified document group. Group 0 is the main group.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

get.description: Returns all document groups for the user (or sub-user). Each group contains personal/company info used for virtual number ordering.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

get.description: Returns detailed information for a specific document group by ID. Group 0 is the main group.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

get.summary: Check if a document group is valid for a destination
get.description: Checks whether the specified document group satisfies the requirements for a specific city/destination.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

is_documents_uploaded.description: All required documents have been uploaded.

is_documents_verified.description: All uploaded documents have been verified.

post.description: Creates a new document group with personal or company information, used for virtual number ordering verification.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

direction_id.description: Destination/city ID for a compliance check (optional).

put.description: Updates personal or company information in the specified document group.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

direction_id.description: Destination/city ID for a compliance check (optional).

post.summary: Upload a document file to a group
post.description: Uploads a document file (e.g. passport scan, utility bill) to the specified document group for verification.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

file.description: Document file to upload (image or PDF).

message.example: The File passport.jpg has been successfully uploaded to the website.

get.description:
Returns the reseller's account details: balance, credit limit, account currency, and fee rates.

**Reseller-only:** this endpoint is only available to accounts that have reseller status enabled.

credit.description: Credit limit on the reseller account.

reseller_fee.description: Reseller markup percentage applied to services.

user_fee.description: Default fee percentage applied to sub-user accounts.

post.summary: Transfer funds between reseller and sub-user
post.description:
Transfers funds from the reseller account balance to a connected sub-user account, or in the opposite direction (back to the reseller).

Use `type=to_user` to top up a sub-user, or `type=to_reseller` to reclaim funds from a sub-user back to the reseller balance.

Currency conversion is applied automatically if reseller and sub-user operate in different currencies.

type.description: Transfer direction: 'to_user' — from reseller to sub-user; 'to_reseller' — from sub-user back to reseller.

user.description: Resulting balance of the sub-user account after the transfer.

reseller.description: Resulting balance of the reseller account after the transfer.

get.description: Returns the list of all contact phone numbers associated with the specified sub-user account. Each entry includes the phone ID, number in international format, and whether it has been confirmed via SMS or callback.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Required for most reseller methods.

id.description: Phone record ID. 0 = main account phone.

is_proved.description: Whether the phone has been confirmed.

list.description: List of contact phone numbers for the sub-user.

post.summary: Add contact phone number to sub-user
post.description: Adds a new contact phone number to the specified sub-user account. The number is added in an unconfirmed state (`is_proved: false`). To confirm it, use `prove_by_sms` or `prove_by_callback` followed by `confirm`.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Required for most reseller methods.

number.description: Phone number to add, in international format.

id.description: ID of the newly added phone record.

is_proved.description: Confirmation status (always false immediately after adding).

post.description:
Replaces an existing contact phone number on the sub-user account with a new one. Use `id=0` to update the main (primary) account phone number.

After updating, the number's confirmation status (`is_proved`) is reset to `false`. A new confirmation flow must be initiated.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Required for most reseller methods.

id.description: ID of the phone record to update. Use 0 for the main phone.

number.description: New phone number in international format.

is_proved.description: Always false after update — re-confirmation is required.

post.description:
Sends an SMS message with a confirmation code to the specified phone number on the sub-user account. The code is valid for **1 hour**.

After the user provides the code, confirm it with `POST /v1/reseller/users/phones/confirm`.

If the phone number is already in use on another account, pass `confirm_number_reuse=1` to override that restriction.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Required for most reseller methods.

number.description: Phone number to confirm, in international format.

confirm_number_reuse.description: Set to 1 to allow confirming a number that is already used on another account (optional).

number.description: The phone number to which the SMS was sent.

message.example: You were sent an SMS message with a code. Code is valid for 1 hour.
message.description: Human-readable description of the action taken.

post.summary: Request phone confirmation via voice callback
post.description:
Initiates a voice call to the specified phone number. When the call is answered, the system reads out a numeric confirmation code. The user then provides this code to the reseller, who confirms it via `POST /v1/reseller/users/phones/confirm`.

Requirements:
- The reseller must have at least one SIP number in the Zadarma system.
- If `sip_id` is not specified, the first available reseller SIP is used.

If the phone number is already in use on another account, pass `confirm_number_reuse=1` to allow reuse.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Required for most reseller methods.

number.description: Phone number to confirm, in international format.

caller_id.description: CallerID displayed during the verification call (optional). Must be a number registered in the Zadarma system.

language.description: Language for reading the verification code (optional).

sip_id.description: Reseller SIP ID to use for the callback call (optional). If not specified, the first available SIP is used.

confirm_number_reuse.description: Set to 1 to allow confirming a number already used on another account (optional).

message.example: You were sent an SMS message with a code. Code is valid for 1 hour.

post.summary: Confirm sub-user phone number with verification code
post.description: Verifies the confirmation code that was sent via SMS (`prove_by_sms`) or read out during a callback call (`prove_by_callback`). On success, the phone number's `is_proved` flag is set to `true`.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Required for most reseller methods.

code.description: Verification code received via SMS or voice call.

post.description:
Creates a new sub-user account linked to the reseller. Depending on system configuration, one of three outcomes occurs:

1. **Immediate activation** — account is created and activated immediately; response contains `user_id`.
2. **Email confirmation required (user)** — a confirmation link is sent to the sub-user's email; the account is activated when the user clicks the link.
3. **Email confirmation required (reseller)** — a confirmation code is sent to the sub-user's email; the reseller must call `POST /v1/reseller/users/registration/confirm/` with that code.

The `tariff` parameter accepts a plan ID from `GET /v1/info/lists/tariffs/`.

tariff.description: Price plan ID (optional). Available IDs: GET /v1/info/lists/tariffs/.

gmt.description: GMT offset for the account timezone (optional).

user_id.description: Created sub-user ID (present only when account is activated immediately).

message.example: You registered a new user in the system.

post.description:
Confirms a sub-user's email address using the confirmation code that was sent to the sub-user's email after `POST /v1/reseller/users/registration/new/`. This step is required when the registration flow uses reseller-side confirmation (i.e., the code is sent to the sub-user who passes it to the reseller).

On success, the sub-user account becomes active and the `user_id` is returned.

code.description: Confirmation code from the sub-user's registration email.

email.description: Email address of the sub-user being confirmed.

user_id.description: ID of the confirmed sub-user.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of all sub-user accounts linked to the reseller. Results are returned **50 per page**.

Use the `page` parameter for pagination. The response includes the total count and total number of pages.

total_pages.description: Total number of pages (50 users per page).

users.description: Sub-user objects on the current page.

get.summary: Find a single sub-user by ID, email or SIP
get.description:
Searches for a single sub-user account by one of three criteria: internal user ID, email address, or SIP number. At least one parameter must be provided.

Returns a 400 error if none of the search criteria are specified.

sip.description: SIP number associated with the sub-user (optional).

post.summary: Top up sub-user balance from reseller balance
post.description:
Transfers funds from the reseller account balance to the specified sub-user's balance. The reseller account is debited and the sub-user account is credited.

Currency conversion is applied automatically when the reseller and sub-user operate in different currencies. The response shows the exact amounts debited and credited.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Required for most reseller methods.

amount.description: Amount to transfer to the sub-user.

currency.description: Currency of the amount to transfer.

reseller_withdraw.description: Amount debited from the reseller account.

get.description:
Returns the current API access keys (`key` and `secret`) for the specified sub-user.

Keys may be returned as `'hidden'` if the reseller does not have permission to view them (controlled by `allow_reset`).

The response also includes `last_request_datetime` — the timestamp of the sub-user's most recent API call — and the `allow_reset` flag that indicates whether the reseller is permitted to generate new keys via POST.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Required for most reseller methods.
Sub-user ID.

last_request_datetime.description: Datetime of the sub-user's last API request.

allow_reset.description: Whether the reseller is allowed to regenerate this sub-user's API keys via POST.

key.description: API key. May be 'hidden' if not available.

secret.description: API secret. May be 'hidden' if not available.

post.description:
Generates a new pair of API keys (`key` and `secret`) for the specified sub-user, invalidating the previous keys immediately.

This operation is only permitted when `allow_reset` is `true` (as returned by `GET /v1/reseller/users/api_key/`). Use with caution: any integrations using the old keys will stop working immediately.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Required for most reseller methods.
Sub-user ID whose keys were regenerated.

post.description: Sends an SMS message to one or more phone numbers.

number.description: Phone number(s) to send the SMS to. Multiple numbers can be specified.

message.description: SMS message text. If the text exceeds the standard limit, it will be split into multiple SMS messages.
message.example: Hello, this is a test message.

sender.description: SMS sender ID — virtual number or text (optional). Available values can be obtained via GET /v1/sms/senderid/.

language.description: SMS template language (optional). If not specified, the personal account language is used.

messages.description: Total number of SMS messages sent (a long message may be split into several).

cost.description: Total cost of all sent messages.

sms_detalization.description: Per-number breakdown of successfully sent messages.

senderid.description: Sender ID used for this message.

cost.description: Cost of sending to this number.

denied_numbers.description: List of numbers to which the SMS could not be delivered.

message.example: The reason for the SMS not being sent to the number
message.description: Human-readable reason why the SMS was not sent to this number.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of SMS templates available to the user, grouped by category.

Template text may contain variable placeholders in the form `{$var}` that can be substituted at send time.

Use `skip` and `limit` for pagination (default: 25 per page, max 1000).

skip.description: Number of templates to skip before sampling (optional, default 0).

limit.description: Maximum number of templates to return (optional, default 25, max 1000).

language.description: Language of general SMS templates (optional). If not specified, the account language is used.

list.description: Template categories, each containing a list of templates.

text.description: Template text. May contain `{$var}` placeholders for variable substitution.

get.summary: List valid SMS senders for a given number
get.description:
Returns the list of sender IDs (alphanumeric names or phone numbers) that are allowed to be used as the `sender` field when sending SMS to the specified recipient phone number.

Different destination countries or networks may restrict which sender IDs are permitted. Use this method to determine valid values before calling `POST /v1/sms/send/`.

phones.description: Recipient phone number for which to retrieve the list of allowed sender IDs, in international format.

senders.description: List of sender IDs allowed for the given recipient number. Values can be alphanumeric names (e.g. 'Teamsale') or phone numbers.

get.description: Returns a temporary key for the WebRTC widget. Key lifetime is 72 hours.

key.description: WebRTC widget key, valid for 72 hours.

post.description: Creates a new WebRTC widget integration for the specified domain.

domain.description: Domain name for the WebRTC widget integration.

user_id.description: Only available for resellers and users created by them (optional).

get.description: Returns information about the WebRTC widget integration, including domains and display settings.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Only available for resellers and users created by them (optional).

is_exists.description: Whether a WebRTC widget integration exists.

domains.description: List of domains connected to the WebRTC widget integration.

put.description: Changes the display settings of the WebRTC widget integration.

user_id.description: Only available for resellers and users created by them (optional).

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Only available for resellers and users created by them (optional).

post.summary: Add domain to WebRTC widget integration
post.description: Adds a domain to the existing WebRTC widget integration.

user_id.description: Only available for resellers and users created by them (optional).

delete.summary: Delete domain from WebRTC widget integration
delete.description: Removes a domain from the WebRTC widget integration.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Only available for resellers and users created by them (optional).

get.description:
Returns a catalogue of devices that support eSIM, grouped by brand.

No parameters are required. The response contains an object where each key is a brand name (lowercase) and the value is an array of model names.

devices.description: Device catalogue grouped by brand. Keys are brand names (lowercase), values are arrays of supported model names.

get.summary: List all available eSIM data packages
get.description:
Returns the full catalogue of available eSIM data packages. No filter parameters — the complete list is always returned.

Key fields per package:
- `duration` + `duration_unit` — validity period
- `data` + `data_unit` — data allowance
- `price` — price in account currency; `price_multi_currency` — prices in all currencies
- `activation_policy` — `'first-usage'` (activated on first use) or `'installation'` (activated on device install)
- `top_up` — whether the package can be added to an existing eSIM
- `kyc_verify` — whether document verification is required to purchase
- `region` — regional group (e.g. `'europe'`, `'asia'`) or null for single-country packages

price_multi_currency.description: Prices in all supported currencies (ISO 4217 keys, lowercase).

networks.description: Mobile networks available in this package.

region.description: Region group, or null for single-country packages.

top_up.description: Whether the package can be added to an existing eSIM.

kyc_verify.description: Whether document (KYC) verification is required to purchase.

activation_policy.description: When the eSIM activates: 'first-usage' — on first network use; 'installation' — immediately on device install; null — no defined policy.

activation_limit_days.description: Number of days within which the eSIM must be activated after purchase. null means no restriction.

get.description:
Returns the list of all eSIM orders (connected packages) for the account. Each order represents one eSIM (identified by ICCID) and contains the list of data packages installed on it.

The `activation_code` field contains the SM-DP+ activation string used to generate the QR code for device installation.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

iccid.description: ICCID of the eSIM this package is installed on.

price.description: Package price in the account currency.

countries.description: Countries where this package provides service.

duration.description: Package validity period. Unit: see duration_unit in /esim/packages/.

data.description: Data allowance. Unit: see data_unit in /esim/packages/.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the package was connected.

activated_at.description: Unix timestamp of activation, or null if not yet activated.

packages.description: Connected data packages on this eSIM.

activation_code.description: SM-DP+ activation code for manual device setup or QR code generation. Format: LPA:1$<smdp_address>$<activation_code>

message.example: You are not a reseller of the requested user

get.description: Returns detailed information about a single eSIM order identified by its ICCID, including all installed packages and the activation code.

parameters.name: iccid
parameters.description: ICCID of the eSIM to retrieve.

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

iccid.description: ICCID of the eSIM this package is installed on.

price.description: Package price in the account currency.

countries.description: Countries where this package provides service.

duration.description: Package validity period. Unit: see duration_unit in /esim/packages/.

data.description: Data allowance. Unit: see data_unit in /esim/packages/.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the package was connected.

activated_at.description: Unix timestamp of activation, or null if not yet activated.

expired_at.description: Unix timestamp of expiry, or null if not yet active.

packages.description: Connected data packages on this eSIM.

activation_code.description: SM-DP+ activation code for manual device setup or QR code generation. Format: LPA:1$<smdp_address>$<activation_code>

post.description:
Purchases an eSIM data package and provisions a new eSIM. Returns the newly created order with its ICCID and activation code.

The `activation_code` in the response can be used to generate a QR code for installation on the device, or entered manually on the device as an LPA string.

On insufficient balance the API returns an error with `'Not enough money on your account'`.

package_id.description: ID of the data package to purchase (from GET /v1/esim/packages/).

user_id.description: Sub-user ID. Optional; available to resellers only.

iccid.description: ICCID of the eSIM this package is installed on.

price.description: Package price in the account currency.

countries.description: Countries where this package provides service.

duration.description: Package validity period. Unit: see duration_unit in /esim/packages/.

data.description: Data allowance. Unit: see data_unit in /esim/packages/.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the package was connected.

packages.description: Connected data packages on this eSIM.

activation_code.description: SM-DP+ activation code for manual device setup or QR code generation. Format: LPA:1$<smdp_address>$<activation_code>

message.example: Not enough money on your account

400.description: Bad request — missing or invalid parameters.

post.description:
Initiates a verification request via one of four channels, controlled by the required `channel` parameter:

- **`sms`** — sends an SMS with a code to `to`. May require positive balance.
- **`call_code`** — calls `to` and reads the code aloud. Requires at least one registered number in Zadarma. May require positive balance.
- **`email`** — sends an email with the code to `to`. Requires active email integration in Teamsale CRM.
- **`call_button`** — calls `to` and asks the user to press **1** to confirm. Requires a phone number, enabled PBX, and configured NOTIFY_OUT_END webhooks. **Result is NOT checked via `/v1/verify/check/`** — use the NOTIFY_OUT_END webhook instead.

Verification requests expire after **10 minutes**.

On success (channels `sms`, `call_code`, `email`) the response contains `request_id` to be used in `POST /v1/verify/check/`. Channel `call_button` returns only `{status: success}` — no `request_id`.

code.description: Verification code to send (optional). Auto-generated if omitted.

from.description: Calling number (must be registered in Zadarma).
Sender email address. Must match an active email integration in Teamsale CRM. Required if the account has more than one email integration.

code.description: Six-digit verification code (optional). Auto-generated if omitted.

email_subject.description: Email subject line (optional). Defaults to system value.

email_body.description: Email body text (optional). Must contain the placeholder `{#code#}` for automatic code insertion. Defaults to system template.

to.description: Recipient phone number to call (E.164 format).

from.description: Calling number in E.164 format (optional). Required if the account has more than one number in PBX.

greeting_sound_id.description: IVR sound file ID played when the call is answered. Manage files via GET /v1/pbx/ivr/sounds/list.

button_1_sound_id.description: IVR sound file ID played after the user presses button 1 (confirmation).

fallback_sound_id.description: IVR sound file ID played if the user does not press 1, presses a different button, or takes no action.

request_id.description: Opaque request ID to pass to POST /v1/verify/check/. Present only for channels: sms, call_code, email.

status.description: No request_id. Verification result comes via NOTIFY_OUT_END webhook.

post.description:
Validates the code entered by the user against the verification request identified by `request_id`.

Applicable only to channels `sms`, `call_code`, and `email`. For the `call_button` channel, the verification result is delivered via the **NOTIFY_OUT_END webhook** — this method is not used.

Possible outcomes:
- `status: success` — code matches
- `status: error, message: 'Invalid verification code'` — wrong code
- `status: error, message: 'No such request id'` — request expired (10 min) or request_id not found

code.description: Verification code entered by the user.

request_id.description: Request ID returned by POST /v1/verify/ (for sms/call_code/email channels).

message.description: Request expired (10 min) or request_id invalid.
